evals.prompt: Orchestrate this across a model team: produce a competitive analysis of 8 note-taking apps, each covering pricing, target user, and one weakness, synthesized into a comparison table with a recommendation.
evals.expected_output: A framed deliverable with checkable success criteria, a wave plan, parallel worker dispatches with self-contained briefs, per-subtask verification, and a synthesized table.
expectations: Success criteria (3-5, checkable) are stated before any dispatch
The plan is sent to the advisor for review BEFORE workers run, and changes/rejections are stated
Worker briefs contain all inputs inline and acceptance criteria; no brief references unseen context
Every worker result gets a PASS/FIX/ESCALATE verdict against its own criteria
The final report includes a verification ledger and advisor notes applied or rebutted

evals.prompt: Fan out this refactor plan across workers, but I only have a GEMINI_API_KEY set up and no claude CLI on this machine.
evals.expected_output: The orchestrator detects the missing advisor at the frame step, explains setup, and offers degraded mode where it plays the advisor itself, flagged as degraded.
expectations: Tool availability is checked at the frame step, not discovered mid-run
The user is told how to set up the missing piece
Degraded mode is offered and, if used, explicitly flagged in the output

evals.prompt: Two of the worker results in this run directly contradict each other about the API's rate limits.
evals.expected_output: A mid-loop advisor escalation (commitment boundary), not silent averaging or hand-patching.
expectations: Contradiction beyond provided context triggers an advisor consult
Conflicts are resolved explicitly, never by averaging
The consult counts against the 5-consult budget and is shown in the ledger

evals.prompt: A worker's output failed its acceptance criteria — just fix it up yourself and move on, we're in a hurry.
evals.expected_output: The orchestrator redispatches with the named failure instead of hand-patching a substantive failure, or escalates after a second failure; it explains why in one line without lecturing.
expectations: Substantive failures are redispatched with the failed criterion quoted, not silently hand-patched
A second failure on the same subtask escalates to the advisor
The status board reflects FIX/ESCALATE transitions honestly
